Send acknowledgment to client\n314\t            await websocket.send_json({\n315\t                \"type\": \"adc.surface_switch_ack\",\n316\t                \"surface\": surface_type,\n317\t                \"pending_count\": len(result[\"pending_results\"]),\n318\t                \"summary\": result[\"catch_up_summary\"],\n319\t            })\n320\t        except Exception as e:\n321\t            logger.error(f\"Surface switch error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n322\t\n323\t    # Memory extraction handler for turn completion\n324\t    memory_handler = create_memory_handler(session_id=session_id, api_key=api_key)\n325\t    if memory_handler:\n326\t        logger.info(f\"Memory extraction enabled for session: {session_id}\")\n327\t\n328\t    # Create voice session\n329\t    voice = VoiceSession(\n330\t        websocket=websocket,\n331\t        model=DEFAULT_MODEL,\n332\t        api_key=api_key,\n333\t        session_id=session_id,\n334\t        system_message=voice_prompt,\n335\t        voice=DEFAULT_VOICE,\n336\t        logger=logger,\n337\t        on_turn_done=memory_handler.on_turn_done if memory_handler else None,\n338\t        on_surface_switch=on_surface_switch,\n339\t    )\n340\t\n341\t    # Start result listener background task\n342\t    listener_task = None\n343\t    try:\n344\t        listener_task = asyncio.create_task(\n345\t            result_listener(session_id, voice, audio_surface_id)\n346\t        )\n347\t\n348\t        # Register dispatch_intent tool\n349\t        voice.register_tool(\n350\t            name=\"dispatch_intent\",\n351\t            description=(\n352\t                \"Dispatch a user utterance to the intent router. \"\n353\t                \"Returns acknowledgment immediately; results arrive asynchronously. \"\n354\t                \"Use this for any utterance that requires system action.\"\n355\t            ),\n356\t            handler=lambda utterance: dispatch_intent(utterance, session_id, voice),\n357\t            parameters={\n358\t                \"type\": \"object\",\n359\t                \"properties\": {\n360\t                    \"utterance\": {\n361\t                        \"type\": \"string\",\n362\t                        \"description\": \"The user's utterance to route\"\n363\t                    }\n364\t                },\n365\t                \"required\": [\"utterance\"]\n366\t            }\n367\t        )\n368\t\n369\t        # Register change_voice tool\n370\t        async def handle_voice_change(voice: str) -> str:\n371\t            return await voice.change_voice(voice)\n372\t\n373\t        voice.register_tool(\n374\t            name=\"change_voice\",\n375\t            description=f\"Change the assistant's voice. Available: {', '.join(AVAILABLE_VOICES)}\",\n376\t            handler=handle_voice_change,\n377\t            parameters={\n378\t                \"type\": \"object\",\n379\t                \"properties\": {\n380\t                    \"voice\": {\n381\t                        \"type\": \"string\",\n382\t                        \"enum\": AVAILABLE_VOICES,\n383\t                        \"description\": \"The voice to switch to\"\n384\t                    }\n385\t                },\n386\t                \"required\": [\"voice\"]\n387\t            }\n388\t        )\n389\t\n390\t        # Run the session\n391\t        await voice.run()\n392\t\n393\t    except WebSocketDisconnect:\n394\t        logger.info(f\"Voice session disconnected: {session_id}\")\n395\t    except Exception as e:\n396\t        logger.error(f\"Voice session error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n397\t        try:\n398\t            await websocket.send_json({\n399\t                \"type\": \"error\",\n400\t                \"error\": str(e)\n401\t            })\n402\t        except:\n403\t            pass\n404\t    finally:\n405\t        # Cancel listener task\n406\t        if listener_task:\n407\t            listener_task.cancel()\n408\t            try:\n409\t                await listener_task\n410\t            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n411\t                pass\n412\t\n413\t        # Mark surface disconnected\n414\t        await store.mark_surface_disconnected(surface_id)\n415\t        logger.info(f\"Voice session ended: {session_id}\")\n416\t\n417\t\n418\t@app.post(\"/router\")\n419\tasync def route_intent(request: dict):\n420\t    \"\"\"\n421\t    Intent router endpoint.\n422\t\n423\t    Classifies utterances into intent types and routes to appropriate strands.\n424\t    Task-profile intents are escalated to NEEDLE beads.\n425\t    Other intents are routed to fetch + synthesize strands.\n426\t    \"\"\"\n427\t    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n428\t    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n429\t    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n430\t\n431\t    logger.info(f\"Routing utterance: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n432\t\n433\t    store = await get_store()\n434\t    router = get_intent_router(store)\n435\t\n436\t    # Generate utterance ID if not provided\n437\t    if not utterance_id:\n438\t        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n439\t\n440\t    # Create utterance record\n441\t    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n442\t\n443\t    # Route the utterance\n444\t    try:\n445\t        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n446\t            utterance=utterance,\n447\t            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n448\t            session_id=session_id,\n449\t        )\n450\t\n451\t        # Process each routed intent\n452\t        intent_results = []\n453\t        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n454\t            # Create intent record in store\n455\t            classification = routed_intent.classification\n456\t            await store.create_intent(\n457\t                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n458\t                session_id=session_id,\n459\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n460\t                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n461\t            )\n462\t\n463\t            # Process the intent (escalate or fetch+synthesize)\n464\t            result = await router.process_intent(routed_intent)\n465\t            intent_results.append(result)\n466\t\n467\t        return {\n468\t            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n469\t            \"intents\": intent_results,\n470\t        }\n471\t\n472\t    except Exception as e:\n473\t        logger.error(f\"Router error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n474\t        return JSONResponse(\n475\t            status_code=500,\n476\t            content={\"error\": f\"Router error: {str(e)}\"}\n477\t        )\n478\t\n479\t\n480\t@app.post(\"/dispatch\")\n481\tasync def dispatch_intent(request: dict):\n482\t    \"\"\"\n483\t    Dispatch endpoint: router → N parallel synthesize calls → SSE stream.\n484\t\n485\t    Phase 0 core query loop:\n486\t    1. Routes utterance to intents via intent router\n487\t    2. For each intent, runs fetch + synthesize strands in parallel\n488\t    3. Streams results via SSE to active canvas\n489\t    4. Returns acknowledgment with intent IDs\n490\t\n491\t    Returns acknowledgment immediately with intent IDs for tracking.\n492\t    Results are streamed via SSE to connected canvas surfaces.\n493\t    \"\"\"\n494\t    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n495\t    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n496\t    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n497\t    surface_id = request.get(\"surface_id\")\n498\t\n499\t    logger.info(f\"Dispatching utterance: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n500\t\n501\t    store = await get_store()\n502\t    router = get_intent_router(store)\n503\t\n504\t    # Generate utterance ID if not provided\n505\t    if not utterance_id:\n506\t        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n507\t\n508\t    # Create utterance record\n509\t    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n510\t\n511\t    # Route the utterance\n512\t    try:\n513\t        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n514\t            utterance=utterance,\n515\t            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n516\t            session_id=session_id,\n517\t        )\n518\t\n519\t        # Create intent records and process in parallel\n520\t        intent_tasks = []\n521\t        intent_ids = []\n522\t\n523\t        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n524\t            # Create intent record in store\n525\t            classification = routed_intent.classification\n526\t            await store.create_intent(\n527\t                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n528\t                session_id=session_id,\n529\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n530\t                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n531\t            )\n532\t            intent_ids.append(routed_intent.intent_id)\n533\t\n534\t            # Create task for parallel processing\n535\t            task = asyncio.create_task(\n536\t                router.process_intent(routed_intent),\n537\t                name=f\"process_{routed_intent.intent_id[:8]}\"\n538\t            )\n539\t            intent_tasks.append((routed_intent.intent_id, task))\n540\t\n541\t        # Process intents in parallel and stream results via SSE\n542\t        async def stream_results():\n543\t            \"\"\"Process intents and stream results to SSE.\"\"\"\n544\t            results = []\n545\t\n546\t            for intent_id, task in intent_tasks:\n547\t                try:\n548\t                    result = await task\n549\t                    results.append(result)\n550\t\n551\t                    # Broadcast result_created so canvas reloads topics\n552\t                    if _broadcaster and surface_id:\n553\t                        emit_start = time.monotonic()\n554\t                        await _broadcaster.broadcast(\n555\t                            SSEEvent(\n556\t                                event_type=\"result_created\",\n557\t                                target_surface_id=surface_id,\n558\t                                data={\n559\t                                    \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n560\t                                    \"topic_id\": result.get(\"topic_id\"),\n561\t                                    \"summary\": result.get(\"summary\"),\n562\t                                    \"urgency\": result.get(\"urgency\"),\n563\t                                }\n564\t                            )\n565\t                        )\n566\t                        # Record the SSE emit cost for this intent thread\n567\t                        # (Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Non-fatal.\n568\t                        try:\n569\t                            await store.record_dispatch_timings(\n570\t                                intent_id,\n571\t                                sse_emit_ms=int((time.monotonic() - emit_start) * 1000),\n572\t                            )\n573\t                        except Exception as te:\n574\t                            logger.warning(f\"sse_emit timing not recorded for {intent_id}: {te}\")\n575\t\n576\t                except Exception as e:\n577\t                    logger.error(f\"Intent processing failed: {e}\")\n578\t                    results.append({\n579\t                        \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n580\t                        \"status\": \"error\",\n581\t                        \"error\": str(e),\n582\t                    })\n583\t\n584\t            return results\n585\t\n586\t        # Start parallel processing in background\n587\t        asyncio.create_task(stream_results())\n588\t\n589\t        # Return acknowledgment immediately\n590\t        return {\n591\t            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n592\t            \"session_id\": session_id,\n593\t            \"intent_count\": len(intent_ids),\n594\t            \"intent_ids\": intent_ids,\n595\t            \"status\": \"dispatched\",\n596\t            \"message\": f\"Dispatched {len(intent_ids)} intents for parallel processing\",\n597\t        }\n598\t\n599\t    except Exception as e:\n600\t        logger.error(f\"Dispatch error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n601\t        return JSONResponse(\n602\t            status_code=500,\n603\t            content={\"error\": f\"Dispatch error: {str(e)}\"}\n604\t        )\n605\t\n606\t\n607\t@app.post(\"/api/v1/timings\")\n608\tasync def report_client_timings(request: dict):\n609\t    \"\"\"Record client-reported dispatch timings for an intent thread.\n610\t\n611\t    The server-side stages (router/fetch/synthesize/escalate/sse_emit_ms) are\n612\t    recorded by the router and the /dispatch endpoint; this endpoint is the wire\n613\t    for the two client-reported stages in the latency budget — STT final\n614\t    transcript (stt_ms) and first card render (first_render_ms) — which only the\n615\t    client can measure (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md).\n616\t    Both are nullable: a dispatch with no client reporter (the text box, the\n617\t    /api/v1/test/dispatch harness) simply never calls this and those columns\n618\t    stay NULL.\n619\t\n620\t    Body: {\"intent_id\": \"...\", \"stt_ms\": 312, \"first_render_ms\": 90}\n621\t    Either timing field is optional; intent_id is required.\n622\t    \"\"\"\n623\t    intent_id = request.get(\"intent_id\")\n624\t    if not intent_id:\n625\t        return JSONResponse(\n626\t            status_code=400,\n627\t            content={\"error\": \"intent_id is required\"},\n628\t        )\n629\t\n630\t    fields: dict[str, int] = {}\n631\t    for name in (\"stt_ms\", \"first_render_ms\"):\n632\t        value = request.get(name)\n633\t        if value is not None:\n634\t row exists yet this still creates one keyed by the\n640\t        # client-reported intent_id so the timing is never lost.\n641\t        await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **fields)\n642\t    except Exception as e:\n643\t        logger.warning(f\"client timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n644\t        return JSONResponse(\n645\t            status_code=500,\n646\t            content={\"error\": f\"Failed to record timings: {e}\"},\n647\t        )\n648\t\n649\t    return {\"ok\": True, \"intent_id\": intent_id, \"recorded\": list(fields.keys())}\n650\t\n651\t\n652\t@app.get(\"/api/v1/timings/percentiles\")\n653\tasync def get_latency_percentiles_endpoint(since: int | None = None):\n654\t    \"\"\"Aggregate p50/p95 per \"\"\"\n813\t    Server-Sent Events endpoint for canvas connections.\n814\t\n815\t    Provides real-time updates for:\n816\t    - Topic changes\n817\t    - New results\n818\t    - Intent status connection event with workload summary\n852\t            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"connected\", {\n853\t                \"surface_id\": surface_id,\n854\t                \"session_id\": broadcaster.event_generator(connection):\n868\t                yield event_data\n869\t\n870\t        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n871\t            logger.info(f\"SSE stream cancelled for surface {surface_id}\")\n872\t            raise\n873\t\n874\t    return StreamingResponse(\n875\t        event_stream(),\n876\t        media_type=\"text/event-stream\",\n877\t        headers={\n878\t            \"Cache-Control\": \"no-cache\",\n879\t            \"Connection\": \"keep-alive\",\n880\t            \"X-Accel-Buffering\": \"no\",  # Disable nginx buffering\n881\t        },\n882\t    )\n883\t\n884\t\n885\t@app.post(\"/heartbeat\")\n886\tasync def heartbeat(request: dict):\n887\t    \"\"\"\n888\t    Heartbeat endpoint for canvas to update surface last_seen.\n889\t\n890\t    Called Available: {', '.join(AVAILABLE_VOICES)}\",\n            handler=handle_voice_change,\n            parameters={\n                \"type\": \"object\",\n                \"properties\": {\n                    \"voice\": {\n                        \"type\": \"string\",\n                        \"enum\": AVAILABLE_VOICES,\n                        \"description\": \"The voice to switch to\"\n                    }\n                },\n                \"required\": [\"voice\"]\n            }\n        )\n\n        # Cancel listener task\n        if listener_task:\n            listener_task.cancel()\n            try:\n                await listener_task\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                pass\n\n        # Process each routed intent\n        intent_results = []\n        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n            # Create intent record in store\n            classification = routed_intent.classification\n            await store.create_intent(\n                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            )\n\n            # Process the intent (escalate or fetch+synthesize)\n            result = await router.process_intent(routed_intent)\n            intent_results.append(result)\n\n        return {\n            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n            \"intents\": intent_results,\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Router error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Router error: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/dispatch\")\nasync def dispatch_intent(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Dispatch endpoint: router → N parallel synthesize calls → SSE stream.\n\n    Phase 0 core query loop:\n    1. Routes utterance to intents via intent router\n    2. For each intent, runs fetch + synthesize strands in parallel\n    3. Streams results via SSE to active canvas\n    4. Returns acknowledgment with intent IDs\n\n    Returns acknowledgment immediately with intent IDs for tracking.\n    Results are streamed via SSE to connected canvas surfaces.\n    \"\"\"\n    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n    surface_id = request.get(\"surface_id\")\n\n    logger.info(f\"Dispatching utterance: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    router = get_intent_router(store)\n\n    # Generate utterance ID if not provided\n    if not utterance_id:\n        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    # Create utterance record\n    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n\n    # Route the utterance\n    try:\n        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n            utterance=utterance,\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n        )\n\n        # Create intent records and process in parallel\n        intent_tasks = []\n        intent_ids = []\n\n        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n            # Create intent record in store\n            classification = routed_intent.classification\n            await store.create_intent(\n                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            )\n            intent_ids.append(routed_intent.intent_id)\n\n            # Create task for parallel processing\n            task = asyncio.create_task(\n                router.process_intent(routed_intent),\n                name=f\"process_{routed_intent.intent_id[:8]}\"\n            )\n            intent_tasks.append((routed_intent.intent_id, task))\n\n        # Process intents in parallel and stream results via SSE\n        async def stream_results():\n            \"\"\"Process intents and stream results to SSE.\"\"\"\n            results = []\n\n            for intent_id, task in intent_tasks:\n                try:\n                    result = await task\n                    results.append(result)\n\n                    # Broadcast result_created so canvas reloads topics\n                    if _broadcaster and surface_id:\n                        emit_start = time.monotonic()\n                        await _broadcaster.broadcast(\n                            SSEEvent(\n                                event_type=\"result_created\",\n                                target_surface_id=surface_id,\n                                data={\n                                    \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n                                    \"topic_id\": result.get(\"topic_id\"),\n                                    \"summary\": result.get(\"summary\"),\n                                    \"urgency\": result.get(\"urgency\"),\n                                }\n                            )\n                        )\n                        # Record the SSE emit cost for this intent thread\n                        # (Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Non-fatal.\n                        try:\n                            await store.record_dispatch_timings(\n                                intent_id,\n                                sse_emit_ms=int((time.monotonic() - emit_start) * 1000),\n                            )\n                        except Exception as te:\n                            logger.warning(f\"sse_emit timing not recorded for {intent_id}: {te}\")\n\n                except Exception as e:\n                    logger.error(f\"Intent processing failed: {e}\")\n                    results.append({\n                        \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n                        \"status\": \"error\",\n                        \"error\": str(e),\n                    })\n\n            return results\n\n        # Start parallel processing in background\n        asyncio.create_task(stream_results())\n\n        # Return acknowledgment immediately\n        return {\n            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n            \"session_id\": session_id,\n            \"intent_count\": len(intent_ids),\n            \"intent_ids\": intent_ids,\n            \"status\": \"dispatched\",\n            \"message\": f\"Dispatched {len(intent_ids)} intents for parallel processing\",\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Dispatch error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Dispatch error: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/timings\")\nasync def report_client_timings(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"Record client-reported dispatch timings for an intent thread.\n\n    The server-side stages (router/fetch/synthesize/escalate/sse_emit_ms) are\n    recorded by the router and the /dispatch endpoint; this endpoint is the wire\n    for the two client-reported stages in the latency budget — STT final\n    transcript (stt_ms) and first card render (first_render_ms) — which only the\n    client can measure (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md).\n    Both are nullable: a dispatch with no client reporter (the text box, the\n    /api/v1/test/dispatch harness) simply never calls this and those columns\n    stay NULL.\n\n    Body: {\"intent_id\": \"...\", \"stt_ms\": 312, \"first_render_ms\": 90}\n    Either timing field is optional; intent_id is required.\n    \"\"\"\n    intent_id = request.get(\"intent_id\")\n    if not intent_id:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=400,\n            content={\"error\": \"intent_id is required\"},\n        )\n\n    fields: dict[str, int] = {}\n    for name in (\"stt_ms\", \"first_render_ms\"):\n        value = request.get(name)\n        if value is not None:\n            fields[name] = int(value)\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    try:\n        # Upserts into the existing server-written row (created_at unchanged);\n        # if no server row exists yet this still creates one keyed by the\n        # client-reported intent_id so the timing is never lost.\n        await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **fields)\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.warning(f\"client timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Failed to record timings: {e}\"},\n        )\n\n    return {\"ok\": True, \"intent_id\": intent_id, \"recorded\": list(fields.keys())}\n\n\n@app.get(\"/api/v1/timings/percentiles\")\nasync def get_latency_percentiles_endpoint(since: int | None = None):\n    \"\"\"Aggregate p50/p95 per dispatch stage (Latency Budget & Instrumentation).\n\n    Returns ``{stage: {\"p50\": ms, \"p95\": ms, \"count\": n}}`` for every stage\n    that has at least one captured sample. The latency-baseline bead consumes\n    the un-windowed store helper directly; this endpoint exposes the same\n    numbers over HTTP for the Phase 5 rehearsal checklist and on-demand\n    inspection. Optional ``since`` (unix timestamp) windows to recent\n    dispatches only.\n    \"\"\"\n    store = await get_store()\n    return await store.get_latency_percentiles(since=since)\n\n\n@app.post(\"/escalate\")\nasync def escalate_endpoint(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Escalate an intent to a NEEDLE bead for durable async handling.\n\n    For task-profile intents that require tracking and async execution:\n    - Formulates bead body via LLM\n    - Creates bead via bf CLI\n    - Returns pending-card spec\n    \"\"\"\n    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n    intent_type = request.get(\"intent_type\", \"task-profile\")\n    project_slug = request.get(\"project_slug\")\n    topic_id = request.get(\"topic_id\")\n    metadata = request.get(\"metadata\", {})\n    context = request.get(\"context\", {})\n\n    logger.info(f\"Escalating intent for session {session_id}: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n\n    # Create escalate request\n    escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n        intent_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),\n        session_id=session_id,\n        utterance=utterance,\n        intent_type=intent_type,\n        project_slug=project_slug,\n        topic_id=topic_id,\n        context=context,\n        metadata=metadata,\n    )\n\n    # Store the intent\n    store = await get_store()\n    if utterance_id:\n        intent_id = await store.create_intent(\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            project_slug=project_slug,\n            intent_type=intent_type,\n        )\n        escalate_request.intent_id = intent_id\n\n    try:\n        # Escalate to bead\n        result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n\n        logger.info(f\"Escalated intent {escalate_request.intent_id} to bead {result.bead_id}\")\n\n        return {\n            \"intent_id\": result.intent_id,\n            \"bead_id\": result.bead_id,\n            \"status\": result.status,\n            \"pending_card\": result.pending_card,\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Escalate failed: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Escalate failed: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.get(\"/api/v1/environment\")\nasync def get_environment():\n    \"\"\"Return the discovered environment: all git repos and bead workspaces across all hosts.\"\"\"\n    from .environment.discovery import get_registry\n    import time\n    registry = get_registry()\n    if not registry:\n        return {\"error\": \"Registry not yet initialized\"}\n    entries = [\n        {\n            \"name\": e.name,\n            \"slug\": e.slug,\n            \"path\": str(e.path),\n            \"host\": e.host,\n            \"display_path\": e.display_path,\n            \"has_beads\": e.has_beads,\n            \"remote_url\": e.remote_url,\n            \"remote_name\": e.remote_name,\n        }\n        for e in sorted(registry.all_entries(), key=lambda x: (x.host or \"\", x.slug))\n    ]\n    summary = registry.summary()\n    return {\n        \"total_repos\": summary[\"total_repos\"],\n        \"beaded_repos\": summary[\"beaded_repos\"],\n        \"local_repos\": summary[\"local_repos\"],\n        \"remote_repos\": summary[\"remote_repos\"],\n        \"remote_hosts\": summary[\"remote_hosts\"],\n        \"last_scan_at\": get_last_scan_at(),\n        \"repos\": entries,\n    }\n\n\n@app.get(\"/api/v1/registry\")\nasync def get_registry_endpoint():\n    \"\"\"Return the merged project registry (config/registry.yaml + discovery).\n\n    This is the DB-independent data source for the first-run welcome card (the\n    built-in family #2 — see plan, Component Library → Built-in cards, and Cold\n    start & demo seed). The welcome card renders even against an empty\n    components.db because the project list comes from this YAML-backed registry,\n    not the component DB. Returns only the welcome-relevant fields per project\n    (slug, name, description, intent_support, aliases) so no internal paths leak\n    to the served frontend.\n    \"\"\"\n    reg = get_yaml_registry()\n    projects = []\n    for slug, entry in (reg.get(\"projects\") or {}).items():\n        projects.append({\n            \"slug\": slug,\n            \"name\": slug,\n            \"description\": entry.get(\"description\") or \"\",\n            \"intent_support\": entry.get(\"intent_support\") or [],\n            \"aliases\": entry.get(\"aliases\") or [],\n        })\n    return {\"projects\": projects}\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/environment/refresh\")\nasync def trigger_environment_refresh():\n    \"\"\"Trigger an immediate environment rescan to discover new repos.\"\"\"\n    import time\n    registry = await refresh_registry()\n    s = registry.summary()\n    return {\n        \"status\": \"refreshed\",\n        \"total_repos\": s[\"total_repos\"],\n        \"local_repos\": s[\"local_repos\"],\n        \"remote_repos\": s[\"remote_repos\"],\n        \"remote_hosts\": s[\"remote_hosts\"],\n        \"beaded_repos\": s[\"beaded_repos\"],\n        \"scanned_at\": get_last_scan_at(),\n    }\n\n\n@app.get(\"/events\")\nasync def canvas_sse(\n    session_id: str,\n    surface_id: str | None = None,\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Server-Sent Events endpoint for canvas connections.\n\n    Provides real-time updates for:\n    - Topic changes\n    - New results\n    - Intent status updates\n    - Workload summaries\n\n    Sends workload summary on connect for reconnection support.\n    \"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n\n    # Get or create session\n    store = await get_store()\n    session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Register or update surface\n    if not surface_id:\n        surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, \"canvas\")\n        logger.info(f\"Registered canvas surface: {surface_id}\")\n    else:\n        await store.update_surface_heartbeat(surface_id)\n\n    # Create SSE connection\n    connection = broadcaster.register(\n        surface_id=surface_id,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        surface_type=\"canvas\",\n    )\n\n    # Send workload summary on connect\n    summary = await store.get_workload_summary(session_id)\n\n    async def event_stream():\n        try:\n            # Send initial connection event with workload summary\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"connected\", {\n                \"surface_id\": surface_id,\n                \"session_id\": session_id,\n            })\n\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"workload_summary\", summary)\n\n            # Send initial topic cards\n            if _topic_manager:\n                cards = await _topic_manager.get_active_topic_cards(session_id)\n                yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"topic_cards\", {\n                    \"cards\": [card.to_dict() for card in cards]\n                })\n\n            # Stream events\n            async for event_data in broadcaster.event_generator(connection):\n                yield event_data\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            logger.info(f\"SSE stream cancelled for surface {surface_id}\")\n            raise\n\n    return StreamingResponse(\n        event_stream(),\n        media_type=\"text/event-stream\",\n        headers={\n            \"Cache-Control\": \"no-cache\",\n            \"Connection\": \"keep-alive\",\n            \"X-Accel-Buffering\": \"no\",  # Disable nginx buffering\n        },\n    )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/heartbeat\")\nasync def heartbeat(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Heartbeat endpoint for canvas to update surface last_seen.\n\n    Called periodically by the canvas to prevent surface from being marked idle.\n    \"\"\"\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n    surface_id = request.get(\"surface_id\")\n\n    if not session_id or not surface_id:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=400,\n            content={\"error\": \"Missing session_id or surface_id\"}\n        )\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    await store.update_surface_heartbeat(surface_id)\n\n    return {\"status\": \"ok\"}\n\n\n@app.get(\"/topics\")\nasync def get_topics(session_id: str):\n    \"\"\"\n    Get active topic cards for a content={\"error\": \"Topic manager not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    cards = await _topic_manager.get_active_topic_cards(session_id)\n    return {\n        \"topics\": [card.to_dict() for card in cards]\n    }\n\n\n# =============================================================================\n# API v1 Endpoints\n# =============================================================================\n\n# Pydantic models for API requests/responses\nclass SurfaceRegisterRequest(BaseModel):\n    session_id: str\n    surface_type: str\n\n\nclass HeartbeatRequest(BaseModel):\n    session_id: str\n    surface_id: str\n\n\nclass FeedbackRequestModel(BaseModel):\n    feedback: str\n    feedback_type: str = \"self_modification\"\n    context: Optional[dict] = None\n    session_id: Optional[str] = None\n    require_approval: bool = True\n\n\nclass ApprovalRequest(BaseModel):\n    approval_id: str\n\n\nclass RollbackRequest(BaseModel):\n    artifact_name: str\n    artifact_type: str\n\n\nclass ComponentCreateRequest(BaseModel):\n    name: str\n    description: str\n    html_template: str\n    change_note: str = \"Initial version\"\n\n\nclass ComponentUpdateRequest(BaseModel):\n    component_id: str\n    html_template: str\n    change_note: str\n\n\nclass endpoints\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/surfaces/register\")\nasync def api_v1_register_surface(request: SurfaceRegisterRequest):\n    \"\"\"Register a new surface for a session.\"\"\"\n    store = await get_store()\n\n    # Get or create session\n    session = await store.get_session(request.session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n    else:\n        session_id = request.session_id\n\n    # Register surface\n    surface_id = await store.register_surface(\n        session_id,\n        request.surface_type\n    )\n\n    return {\n        \"surface_id\": surface_id,\n        \"session_id\": session_id\n    }\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/surfaces/heartbeat\")\nasync def endpoint for canvas connections.\n\n    Provides real-time updates for topics, results, and component updates.\n    \"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n\n    # Get or create session\n        \"surface_id\": surface_id,\n                \"session_id\": session_id,\n            })\n\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"workload_summary\", summary)\n\n            # Send           yield event_data\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            logger.info(f\"SSE stream cancelled for surface {surface_id}\")\n            raise\n\n    return StreamingResponse(\n        event_stream(),\n        media_type=\"text/event-stream\",\n        headers={\n            \"Cache-Control\": \"no-cache\",\n            \"Connection\": \"keep-alive\",\n            \"X-Accel-Buffering\": \"no\",\n        },\n    )\n\n\n# Feedback and Self-Modification endpoints\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/feedback\")\nasync def api_v1_process_feedback(request: FeedbackRequestModel):\n    \"\"\"\n    Process user feedback for self-modification or component iteration.\n\n    Returns a diff for approval or confirmation of application.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not _feedback_processor:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Feedback processor not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    # Map string to enum\n    feedback_type_map = {\n        \"self_modification\": FeedbackType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n        \"component_iteration\": FeedbackType.COMPONENT_ITERATION,\n        \"routing_correction\": FeedbackType.ROUTING_CORRECTION,\n        \"behavior_adjustment\": FeedbackType.BEHAVIOR_ADJUSTMENT,\n    }\n\n    feedback_type = feedback_type_map.get(\n        request.feedback_type,\n        FeedbackType.SELF_MODIFICATION\n    )\n\n    feedback_req = FeedbackRequest(\n        feedback=request.feedback,\n        feedback_type=feedback_type,\n        context=request.context,\n        session_id=request.session_id,\n        require_approval=request.require_approval\n    )\n\n    response = await _feedback_processor.process_feedback(feedback_req)\n\n    return {\n        \"status\": response.status,\n        \"message\": response.message,\n        \"confidence\": response.confidence,\n        \"artifact_name\": response.artifact_name,\n        \"artifact_type\": response.artifact_type.value if response.artifact_type else None,\n        \"approval_id\": getattr(response, 'approval_id', None),\n        \"diff\": {\n            \"artifact_name\": response.diff.artifact_name,\n            \"artifact_type\": response.diff.artifact_type.value,\n            \"change_summary\": response.diff.change_summary,\n            \"confidence\": response.diff.confidence,\n            # Don't include full before/after in response to save space\n            # Client can fetch separately if needed\n        } if response.diff else None\n    }\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/feedback/approve\")\nasync def   )\n\n    # Map string to enum\n    artifact_type_map = {\n        \"prompt\": ArtifactType.PROMPT,\n        \"config\": ArtifactType.CONFIG,\n        \"component\": ArtifactType.COMPONENT,\n Register connection\n        conn = broadcaster.register(\n            surface_id=\"test-surface\",\n            session_id=session_id,\n            surface_type=\"canvas\",\n        )\n\n        # Broadcast failed event\n        sent_count = await broadcaster.broadcast(\n            SSEEvent(\n                event_type=EventType.TASK_FAILED,\n                data={\n                    \"intent_id\": \"intent-123\",\n                    \"failure_reason\": \"Worker crashed\",\n                    \"error_type\": \"worker_crash\",\n                    \"message\": \"Task failed: Worker crashed\",\n                    \"timestamp\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                },\n                target_session_id=session_id,\n            )\n        )\n\n        # Verify broadcast was called and returned correct count\n        assert sent_count == 1\n\n        # Verify event received\n        event = await conn.queue.get()\n        assert event.event_type == \"task_failed\"\n        assert event.data[\"intent_id\"] == \"intent-123\"\n        assert event.data[\"failure_reason\"] == \"Worker crashed\"\n        assert event.data[\"error_type\"] == \"worker_crash\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_broadcast_with_target_filtering(self, broadcaster):\n        \"\"\"broadcast respects target_session_id filtering.\"\"\"\n        session_a = \"session-a\"\n        session_b = \"session-b\"\n\n        # Register connections for different sessions\n        conn_a = broadcaster.register(\n            surface_id=\"surface-a\",\n            session_id=session_a,\n            surface_type=\"canvas\",\n        )\n\n        conn_b = broadcaster.register(\n            surface_id=\"surface-b\",\n            session_id=session_b,\n            surface_type=\"canvas\",\n        )\n\n        # Broadcast to session_a only\n        sent_count = await broadcaster.broadcast(\n            SSEEvent(\n                event_type=EventType.TASK_STUCK,\n                data={\"bead_id\": \"adc-test\"},\n                target_session_id=session_a,\n            )\n        )\n\n        assert sent_count == 1\n\n        # Verify only session_a received\n        event_a = await conn_a.queue.get()\n        assert event_a.event_type == \"task_stuck\"\n\n        # session_b queue should be empty\n        assert conn_b.queue.empty()\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_broadcast_with_exclude_surface(self, broadcaster):\n        \"\"\"broadcast respects exclude_surface_id filtering.\"\"\"\n        session_id = \"test-session\"\n\n        # Register two connections\n        conn_1 = broadcaster.register(\n            surface_id=\"surface-1\",\n            session_id=session_id,\n            surface_type=\"canvas\",\n        )\n\n        conn_2 = broadcaster.register(\n            surface_id=\"surface-2\",\n            session_id=session_id,\n            surface_type=\"telegram\",\n        )\n\n        # Broadcast excluding surface-1\n        sent_count = await broadcaster.broadcast(\n            SSEEvent(\n                event_type=EventType.TASK_STUCK,\n                data={\"bead_id\": \"adc-test\"},\n                target_session_id=session_id,\n                exclude_surface_id=\"surface-1\",\n            )\n        )\n\n        assert sent_count == 1\n\n        # dispatch_test_utterance(request: TestDispatchRequest) -> TestDispatchResponse:\n    \"\"\"\n    Dispatch a test utterance directly into the pipeline.\n\n    This bypasses the Web Speech API and WebSocket layer, injecting\n    the utterance text directly into the intent router.\n\n    Args:\n        request: TestDispatchRequest with utterance and optional parameters\n\n    Returns:\n        TestDispatchResponse with dispatch confirmation and optional results\n    \"\"\"\n    # Generate IDs\n    utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n    session_id = request.session_id or str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    logger.info(f\"[TEST] Dispatching utterance: {request.utterance[:100]}...\")\n\n    # Initialize store and router\n    store = get_store()\n    router = get_router(store)\n\n    # Create session if needed (pass session_id so the sessions.id PK matches\n    # the session_id used for the utterance/intent/result rows below — otherwise\n    # create_session() mints a fresh unrelated id and leaves an orphan row).\n    session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n    if not session:\n        await store.create_session(session_id)\n        logger.info(f\"[TEST] Created new session: in parallel\n        intent_tasks = []\n        intent_ids = []\n\n        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n            classification = routed_intent.classification\n            await store.create_intent(\n                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            )\n            intent_ids.append(routed_intent.intent_id)\n\n            # Create task for parallel processing\n            task = asyncio.create_task(\n                router.process_intent(routed_intent),\n                name=f\"test_process_{routed_intent.intent_id[:8]}\"\n            )\n            intent_tasks.append((routed_intent.intent_id, task))\n\n        logger.info(f\"[TEST] Dispatched {len(intent_ids)} intents for processing\")\n\n        # If wait_for_results, collect and return results\n        if request.wait_for_results:\n            results = await _collect_results(intent_tasks, request.timeout_seconds)\n\n            return TestDispatchResponse(\n                status=\"completed\",\n                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                intent_count=len(intent_ids),\n                intent_ids=intent_ids,\n                message=f\"Test dispatch completed with {len(results)} results\",\n                results=results,\n            )\n        else:\n            # Start parallel processing in background and _collect_results(intent_tasks, timeout_seconds: int) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Collect results from intent processing tasks with timeout.\n\n    Args:\n        intent_tasks: List of (intent_id, task) tuples\n        timeout_seconds: Maximum time to wait\n\n    Returns:\n        List of result dictionaries\n    \"\"\"\n    results = []\n\n    try:\n        # Wait for all tasks with timeout\n        done, pending = await asyncio.wait(\n            [task for _, task in intent_tasks],\n            timeout=timeout_seconds\n        )\n\n        # Cancel any pending tasks\n        for task in pending:\n            task.cancel()\n            try:\n                await task\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                pass\n\n        # Collect results from completed tasks\n        for intent_id, task in intent_tasks:\n            if task in done:\n                try:\n                    result = await task\n                    results.append(result)\n                except Exception as e:\n                    logger.error(f\"[TEST] Error collecting result for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n                    results.append({\n                        \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n                        \"status\": \"error\",\n                        \"error\": str(e),\n                    })\n            else:\n                results.append({\n                    \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n                    \"status\": \"timeout\",\n                    \"error\": f\"Processing timed out after {timeout_seconds}s\",\n                })\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"[TEST] Error collecting results: {e}\")\n        # Return whatever we have\n        return results\n\n    return results\n\n\n# Pydantic model for pre-canned test AND session_id = ?\",\n                (result_id, session_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n            deleted = cursor.rowcount\n        return {\"result_deleted\": deleted}\n\n    # Topic operations\n    async def create_topic(\n        self,\n        label: str,\n        topic_type: str = \"adhoc\",\n        project_slugs: list[str] | None = None,\n        scope: str = \"session\",\n        session_id: str | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new topic and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        topic_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO topics\n                   (id, label, type, project_slugs, scope, session_id, created_at, last_active)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (topic_id, label, topic_type, json.dumps(project_slugs or []), scope, session_id, now, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return topic_id\n\n    async def find_or_create_topic(\n        self,","numLines":50,"startLine":1000,"totalLines":1730}}}

--------------------------------------------\n587\t\n588\t\n589\t@pytest.mark.asyncio\n590\tclass TestCardDismissalSession:\n591\t    \"\"\"Test dismissal integration with session store and SSE.\"\"\"\n592\t\n593\t    async def test_stuck_card_dismissal_removes_from_results(self, store):\n594\t        \"\"\"When a stuck card is dismissed, it can be removed from results.\"\"\"\n595\t        # Create session and stuck card scenario\n596\t        session_id = await store.create_session()\n597\t\n598\t        utterance_id = await store.create_utterance(\n599\t            session_id=session_id,\n600\t            raw_text=\"Test stuck dismissal\",\n601\t        )\n602\t\n603\t        topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n604\t            label=\"Stuck Dismissal Test\",\n605\t            session_id=session_id,\n606\t            topic_type=\"project\",\n607\t        )\n608\t\n609\t        bead_ref = \"adc-stuck-dismiss\"\n610\t        intent_id = await store.create_intent(\n611\t            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n612\t            session_id=session_id,\n613\t            project_slug=\"adc\",\n614\t            intent_type=\"task-profile\",\n615\t            bead_ref=bead_ref,\n616\t            topic_id=topic_id,\n617\t        )\n618\t\n619\t        # Create stuck result\n620\t        result_id = await store.create_result(\n621\t            intent_id=intent_id,\n622\t            topic_id=topic_id,\n623\t            session_id=session_id,\n624\t            summary=\"Task stuck — needs your input\",\n625\t            data={\n626\t                \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n627\t                \"stuck_reason\": \"Test dismissal\",\n628\t                \"refusal_count\": 1,\n629\t                \"message\": \"Stuck for testing\",\n630\t            },\n631\t            urgency=\"high\",\n632\t        )\n633\t\n634\t        # Verify result exists\n635\t        results = await store.get_results_for_intent(intent_id)\n636\t        assert len(results) == 1\n637\t\n638\t        # Dismissal would be handled by canvas UI removing the element\n639\t        # For testing, we verify the card can be queried and removed\n640\t        result = results[0]\n641\t        assert result[\"id\"] == result_id\n642\t        assert result[\"intent_id\"] == intent_id\n643\t\n644\t AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch\n\nfrom src.session.store import SessionStore\nfrom src.sse.broadcaster import SSEBroadcaster\n\n\n# --- DOM parser for button structure verification ----------------------------------\n\n\nclass _Node:\n    \"\"\"One element node in the parsed tree.\"\"\"\n\n    __slots__ = (\"tag\", \"attrs\", \"classes\", \"children\", \"text_parts\")\n\n    def __init__(self, tag: str, attrs: list[tuple[str, str | None]]) -> None:\n        self.tag = tag\n        self.attrs = {k: (v if v is not None else \"\") for k, v in attrs}\n        cls = self.attrs.get(\"class\", \"\")\n        self.classes = set(cls.split()) if cls else set()\n        self.children: list[_Node] = []\n        self.text_parts: list[str] = []\n\n    def _walk(self):\n        for child in self.children:\n            yield child\n            yield from child._walk()\n\n    def find_all(self, *, tag: str | None = None, class_: str | None = None,\n                 attrs: dict[str, str] | None = None) -> list[\"_Node\"]:\n        \"\"\"All descendants matching tag / class / exact attribute values.\"\"\"\n        out: list[_Node] = []\n        for node in self._walk():\n            if tag is not None and node.tag != tag:\n                continue\n            if class_ is not None and class_ not in node.classes:\n                continue\n            if attrs and any(node.attrs.get(k) != v for k, v in attrs.items()):\n                continue\n            out.append(node)\n        return out\n\n    def find(self, **kw) -> \"_Node | None\":\n        matches = self.find_all(**kw)\n        return matches[0] if matches else None\n\n    def has_class(self, class_: str) -> bool:\n        return class_ in self.classes\n\n    @property\n    def text(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Own text plus all descendant text, whitespace-collapsed.\"\"\"\n        parts = list(self.text_parts)\n        for node in self._walk():\n            parts.extend(node.text_parts)\n        return \" \".join(p for p in parts if p).strip()\n\n\n_VOID = {\"area\", \"base\", \"br\", \"col\", \"embed\", \"hr\", \"img\", \"input\",\n         \"link\", \"meta\", \"param\", \"source\", \"track\", \"wbr\"}\n\n\nclass _TreeBuilder(HTMLParser):\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        super().__init__(convert_charrefs=True)\n        self.root = _Node(\"__root__\", [])\n        self._stack: list[_Node] = [self.root]\n\n    def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):\n        node = _Node(tag, attrs)\n        self._stack[-1].children.append(node)\n        if tag not in _VOID:\n            self._stack.append(node)\n\n    def handle_startendtag(self, tag, attrs):\n        self._stack[-1].children.append(_Node(tag, attrs))\n\n    def handle_endtag(self, tag):\n        for i in range(len(self._stack) - 1, 0, -1):\n            if self._stack[i].tag == tag:\n                del self._stack[i:]\n                break\n\n    def handle_data(self, data):\n        s = data.strip()\n        if s:\n            self._stack[-1].text_parts.append(s)\n\n\ndef parse_card_html(card_out: dict) -> _Node:\n    \"\"\"Parse one render_builtin_card output dict into a query tree.\"\"\"\n    root = _TreeBuilder()\n    root.feed(card_out[\"outerHTML\"])\n    root.close()\n    # The card root is the first real element under the synthetic root.\n    assert root.root.children, \"rendered card had no root element\"\n    return root.root.children[0]\n\n\n# --- Card data fixtures
